Title: Concerns Regarding Negative Marking Policy for Module III Evaluation
Message:
Dear Sir,

I am writing to express serious concern regarding the negative marking policy announced for the upcoming Module III End Module Evaluation.

Applying negative marking across MCQ, MSQ, and NAT sections creates significant anxiety given the breadth and depth of the syllabus. The topics under this module are extensive and conceptually demanding. Penalizing incorrect attempts in every section may disproportionately affect students despite adequate preparation.

In particular, the application of negative marking in Multiple Select Questions and Numerical Answer Type questions raises concerns about fairness. As seen in competitive examinations such as GATE, which is conducted by IIT Guwahati, negative marking is not applied to MSQ and NAT sections. This ensures that students are not penalized for attempting concept-based or calculation-based questions where precision and interpretation are involved. Adopting a similar approach would provide a more balanced and student-friendly evaluation system.

Regarding NAT questions, numerical answers can often be expressed in equivalent formats such as 12, 12.0, or 12.00. It is unclear how the system will evaluate such variations. A minor formatting difference should not result in a loss of marks if the value is mathematically correct. Clarity on the evaluation mechanism would be helpful.

Additionally, the requirement of 30 percent to pass and 70 percent eligibility criteria for placements already creates substantial academic pressure. With negative marking implemented uniformly, the risk of significant score reduction increases further. This may unintentionally filter students rather than assess their true conceptual understanding.

We respectfully request reconsideration of the negative marking policy, particularly for MSQ and NAT sections, or at least a clear clarification regarding evaluation standards for numerical responses.

Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,
A concerned student

This extended break has raised serious concerns among us as students. We were informed that the lectures would be conducted at a consistent and well-structured pace. However, the current pattern does not reflect that commitment. Such long gaps disrupt continuity, affect our learning momentum, and make it difficult to stay aligned with the course objectives.

Consistency is extremely important in any academic program, especially when the syllabus requires steady engagement and regular practice. These irregular intervals create confusion and make it challenging for us to plan our studies effectively.

I would sincerely request clarification on the following:

1. What is the reason for this prolonged gap between lectures?
2. Will there be a revised schedule to ensure consistency moving forward?
3. How will the missed time be compensated to ensure timely completion of the syllabus?

We are fully committed to attending and participating actively, but we also rely on the institution to maintain the promised pace and structure of the program.

I kindly request you to address this matter at the earliest and provide a clear plan going forward.

Thank you for your attention to this issue.

Sincerely,
Tanvi

Title: Concerns Over Exam Length and Time Allocation Fairness
Message:
Dear Masai team,

I am writing to strongly express my dissatisfaction with today’s examination.

The paper consisted of 120 lengthy and complex questions to be completed within 180 minutes. This made it extremely difficult, and realistically impossible, to complete the test properly within the given time. The issue was not preparation or understanding, but the unreasonable volume and depth of the questions relative to the time allowed.

An examination should fairly assess a student’s knowledge and analytical ability. However, the structure of this test appeared to prioritize speed over understanding, which undermines the purpose of academic evaluation. The format raises serious concerns about fairness and balance.

I request that the exam structure and time allocation be formally reviewed to ensure that future assessments are reasonable and aligned with fair academic standards.

I strongly register my displeasure regarding this matter and expect that it will be taken seriously.

Sincerely,
Jayesh Mohite

Title: Decline in Teaching Quality and Course Structure Concerns
Message:
Hi Masai Team,

I joined this course to upgrade myself with new skills. I come from a commerce background without any prior technical or coding knowledge, which made the learning curve steeper.

At the beginning, the course curriculum and teaching methods were quite effective and helped me gain confidence. Unfortunately, from the Datawarehouse for AI modules onward, the overall teaching quality declined significantly. As a group, we often found ourselves uncertain about how the current methodology would benefit us in the long run. This impacted my learning experience, and I eventually had to discontinue attending classes.

In its current form, the program falls short in providing a structured and effective learning environment. The teaching quality and delivery have been disappointing, and I am dissatisfied with the overall structure of the course.

I sincerely request that the concerns mentioned above be reviewed and addressed to enhance the learning experience for future batches. Once improvements are implemented, I would appreciate being considered for enrollment in an upcoming batch, as I had to step away from the current one due to the issues outlined earlier.

Title: Unfair EMI Penalties and Unprofessional Agent Conduct Complaint
Message:
Dear Sir/Madam,

I am writing this regarding the unfair EMI penalties charged on my education loan associated with Masai University and managed by Propel.

As per the loan agreement, the EMI is due before the 5th of every month. In January, due to a minor delay, I was charged a late fee of approximately ₹600–₹700 per day, which I accepted and paid. However, in February, despite having sufficient balance in my bank account before the due date, an additional penalty of approximately ₹2000 was deducted for just two days.

When I tried to make the payment manually on 4th and 5th February through the provided link, the system displayed a message stating that the EMI would be auto-debited and manual payment was not allowed. Therefore, the delay was not due to my negligence but due to the system restriction from Propel’s side.

It is unfair and unreasonable to charge such a high penalty when the payment mode was restricted and the amount was already available in my account. Charging ₹2000 for two days is excessive and not justified.

Additionally, I am highly disappointed with the behavior of the Propel loan agent who communicated with me in an unprofessional and rude manner, even threatening to send an agent to my home. This is not an acceptable way to speak with a client who is regularly paying EMIs.

I request:
	1.	A clear explanation for the excessive penalty charges.
	2.	Immediate refund of the unjustly deducted amount (₹2000 + previous excess charges if applicable).
	3.	Review of the system issue that prevented manual payment.
	4.	Strict action regarding the unprofessional conduct of the concerned agent.

Title: Request for Exam Rescheduling and Certificate Timeline Clarification
Message:
Hello Sangamesh,

I have a concern regarding my AIML program schedule and my university exams.

According to the current Masai schedule, the **Module 3 final exam is on 26 April 2026**, but my **university semester exams are also expected to start around the same time**. The next possible schedule around **3 May** may also overlap because my university exams might continue until **9 May**. In that case, would it be possible for me to give the exam on **10 May or 17 May**?

I understand that these dates are tentative, but I wanted to confirm this in advance so I can plan accordingly.

Could you please help clarify:

1. If there is a clash between my **university exams and the Masai module exam**, is there any possible arrangement or guidance for such cases?
2. When do **internship or placement interviews/tests usually start** — before the final module exam or after the exams are completed?
3. After I complete the course or the final module exam, **when will I receive the final certificate**? I may need to submit it for opportunities with government organizations like **ISRO**, so knowing the expected timeline will help me plan.

It would really help if this information could be cross-verified so I can manage both my university exams and the Masai program properly.

Thank you for your support.

Harsh Yadav

Title: Request for Leniency in Academic Integrity Violation Case
Message:
**Dear Members of the Disciplinary Committee,**

I am writing this letter to formally express my sincere apologies for violating the academic integrity policy during the online evaluation held on Nov 23, 2025 for Module 2 - End Module Evaluation.

I want to candidly admit that I used ChatGPT to assist with certain parts of the assessment. I understand that this action constitutes plagiarism and goes against the values of honesty and hard work that this institution upholds. I deeply regret this lapse in judgment and take full responsibility for my actions.

Regarding the consequences outlined in the plagiarism policy, I am aware that a first occurrence typically results in the entire evaluation attempt being marked as zero. While I respect this policy, I would like to respectfully request a review of my case. I am asking if the committee might consider marking only the specific questions where plagiarism was detected as zero, rather than the entire evaluation.

I have put significant effort into preparing for this course, and I hope that the portions of the exam completed through my own merit might still be considered. I understand that this is a request for leniency, and I am prepared to accept whatever final decision the committee deems appropriate.

Furthermore, I want to assure you that this was an isolated incident. I have reflected on the importance of academic honesty and the long-term value of my own learning process. I guarantee that I will strictly adhere to all guidelines in the future and that such an action will never be repeated.

Thank you for your time and for allowing me the opportunity to explain my actions.

Sincerely, Mohammad Asim

I respectfully request consideration regarding my attendance evaluation and certification.

Before the program began, I informed the team about my professional commitments. Based on this understanding, I joined the program believing recorded sessions completed within the timeline would meet attendance requirements.

Whenever I missed live sessions due to work responsibilities, I ensured all recordings were completed within seven days and regularly studied after work hours to maintain learning continuity.

When attendance expectations later appeared different, I adjusted my work schedule and increased live participation wherever possible.

My Term 2 project, a 36-page independently researched submission, was selected for the Super 8, and I maintained handwritten notes for all lectures.

Considering my onboarding understanding, work dynamics, learning effort, and consistent performance, I humbly request evaluation of my overall commitment and grant of the Completion Certificate instead of a Participation Certificate.

Title: Request for Live Interactive Resume-Building Sessions
Message:
Respected Sir/Madam,

I hope you are doing well.

I am writing to express a concern regarding the resume-building sessions currently being provided. Resume building is a crucial and practical step in our career preparation, and it was communicated earlier that such important topics would be conducted through live, interactive classes.

However, we are being provided only with recorded videos, which appear to be pre-recorded YouTube content. There is no clarity on when these videos were recorded, and hence it is difficult to assess whether the content aligns with current market requirements, latest resume trends, and industry or ATS-friendly standards.

For a topic as important as resume building, recorded sessions do not provide:

Real-time guidance

Personalized feedback

Clarity on current hiring expectations

This approach does not align with what was initially promised. We strongly believe that live sessions are essential for resume building to ensure proper understanding and industry relevance.

We kindly request clarification on this matter and ask that a live resume-building session be arranged as committed.

Thank you for your time and understanding. I look forward to your response.

Yours sincerely,
Varsha

Title: Incorrect evaluation of Graded Assignment 4 probability questions 17, 24, 25
Message:
The Graded Assignment 4 marks are not correct, three of my questions are evaluated incorrectly numbered as 17, 24 and 25. Kindly Re evaluate.
17. A single card is drawn from a standard deck of 52 cards. What is the probability that the card is either a king or a heart?
How is it 17/52, in a deck 13 hearts are there, which includes 1 king as well, remaining 3 kings possible making total of 16 possibilities out of 52, in this questions 2 options are correct 4/13 and 16/52, not sure how 17/52

24. Two cards are drawn from a standard deck without replacement. What is the probability that both are spades?
A standard deck has 52 cards, including 13 spades.Probability the first card is a spade = 13/52 = 1/4
After drawing one spade, 51 cards remain, including 12 spades
Probability the second card is a spade (given the first was a spade) = 12/51 = 4/17 Multiply the probabilities (since the draws are dependent):P(both spades) = (13/52) × (12/51) = (1/4) × (4/17) = 1/17

Alternatively, using combinations (same result):Number of ways to choose 2 spades = C(13,2) = 78 Total ways to choose any 2 cards = C(52,2) = 1326 P = 78 / 1326 = 1/17Final answer: 1/17

25. A fair die is thrown. What is the probability that the number is divisible by 2 given that it is greater than 2?
Explanation (using conditional probability)We want P(divisible by 2 | greater than 2).By definition: P(A|B) = P(A and B) / P(B)Here: A = "number is divisible by 2"
B = "number is greater than 2"

The possible outcomes on a fair six-sided die are: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 (each with probability 1/6).Given that the number is greater than 2, the possible outcomes are reduced to: 3, 4, 5, 6 → There are 4 equally likely outcomes in this conditional space.Among these, the numbers divisible by 2 are: 4 and 6 → There are 2 favorable outcomes.Therefore: P(divisible by 2 | greater than 2) = 2 / 4 = 1/2Final answer: 1/2

Title: Request for One-Year Break Due to Medical Condition and College Commitments
Message:
I am writing to you today to discuss my current journey in the IIT x Masai AI & ML program. While I am deeply passionate about the curriculum and have been putting in my best efforts to learn and grow, I have reached a point where my external circumstances are making it impossible for me to keep up with the course's rigor.
Currently, I am a full-time student with college hours from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM. While I initially felt confident balancing both, I have recently been diagnosed with a serious medical condition involving hypertension. This has led to unpredictable spikes in my heart rate and blood pressure, which are currently being managed with a high dosage of medication. The side effects of the medication, combined with the physical and mental stress of my college schedule, have left me unable to focus or perform at the level this certification demands.
I take my education very seriously and I do not want to continue in a way that compromises the quality of my learning or my health. Because I value this program and want to complete it with full dedication, I would like to request a one-year break or a formal batch deferral.
My goal is to use this time to stabilize my health and complete my current college commitments so that I can return to this course next year and give it 100% of my focus, as a dedicated student should.
I have attached my medical documentation and prescriptions for your reference.
I would be very grateful for your guidance on the official process for this pause. I hope you will understand my situation and support my request so that I can return to the program in the future when I am physically fit to excel.

Title: Clarification Request on Presentation and Demo Format Expectations
Message:
Hi Masai Team,

Thank you for the opportunity to present our project.

We would like to seek clarification regarding the expected format of the presentation. The submission guidelines clearly state we were to "Present a structured deck covering problem, solution, demo and reasoning". Based on this, we prepared a PPT presentation that derives insights from the previously submitted 16-min long demo video and the working application linked in our PRD.

During the session, the focus shifted quickly away from the presentation and towards the demo. Due to the confusion, we were unable to get to the architectural and reasoning portions of our product. As a result, we're concerned that some key aspects of our product that we have worked very hard on may not have been fully communicated.

If a live interactive demo was the expectation in addition to the already submitted materials, we would have gladly prepared for it had it been specified clearly. 

Please provide clarification on this point. We would be happy to provide an additional walkthrough of our product to ensure a complete evaluation of our work.

Thank you for your consideration,
Nayana Anil

Dear Suraj Singh Sir,

I hope you are doing well.

Title: Request for Lecture Download Option Due to Poor Internet Connectivity
Message:
Hello Masai Support Team,

I am currently enrolled in the Masai course and I am very serious about completing the program and attending all lectures regularly.

However, I am facing a genuine issue. I work in a night shift job, so whenever my shift falls at the same time as the live lecture, I watch the lecture recording the next day.

The problem is that the place where I live has very poor internet connectivity. Even 4G does not work properly, and because of this the lectures keep buffering and sometimes they do not play at all. Due to this issue, 5–6 of my lectures are already pending.

If there was an option to download the lecture, I could download it from a place where the network is good and watch it later offline without interruption. This would really help me stay consistent with the course and not fall behind.

I am very committed to completing the course and learning properly. So I kindly request you to please consider providing a lecture download option, or suggest any alternative solution so that students with poor network connectivity can continue learning without issues.

Regards,
Tanvir Shaikh

Title: Module 3 Evaluation Interrupted by Technical and Connectivity Issues
Message:
Dear Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I would like to inform you that while attempting the Module 3 evaluation, I encountered multiple technical issues that affected my ability to complete the assessment successfully. At the initial stage of the evaluation, I faced repeated difficulties connecting to the platform. Even after connecting, the session was interrupted several times due to webcam and proctoring errors, which caused the evaluation to disconnect.

After restarting the system, the webcam issue was temporarily resolved and the evaluation resumed. However, during the assessment the session was again halted multiple times for short durations (approximately 1–2 minutes) due to system or connectivity issues. When I attempted to reconnect and stabilize the internet connection, the webcam error appeared again, which further disrupted the process. As a result, the evaluation could not be completed and the allotted time expired.

I would like to sincerely request your consideration in allowing me another opportunity to attempt the Module 3 evaluation. I assure you that I was attempting the assessment fairly, and the disruption appears to have been caused by system or connectivity-related technical issues beyond my control.

I would be grateful if you could kindly review the situation and permit a reattempt.

Thank you for your understanding and support.

Regards
Abhishek Sharma

I was taking the test from the same place and under the same conditions as my previous attempts, which did not trigger any such problems. Unfortunately, because of these interruptions, I was unable to complete my exam and the session ended prematurely.
This experience was extremely disappointing and stressful. I kindly request that you review my session logs and investigate the proctoring system errors that occurred during my exam. Title: Urgent: Clarification and Support Needed for Unresolved Placement Status
Message:
Dear Masai Placement Team,

I hope this message finds you well.

I am writing again with deep disappointment and concern regarding my placement status. I had previously shared my concerns on 19 August 2025, but unfortunately, my situation remains unchanged even as of 20 February 2026.

I joined Masai School on 20 March 2023 during my 4th year of B.Tech (CSE), with strong hope and trust in building a better career through the Full Stack Development program. Before joining Masai, I had referrals from seniors and even received job offers ranging between ₹15,000–₹18,000 per month. However, I chose to trust Masai’s vision of better opportunities and growth.

Now, after nearly 3 years, I am still unemployed. For the past 18+ months, I have been actively participating in the placement process, yet not a single job offer has been finalized.

There have been multiple instances that have left me extremely discouraged:

* In August 2024, I was selected by Vivid Climate, but I did not receive the offer letter.
* In July 2025, I cleared two technical rounds in a company, but the HR round was never scheduled.
* In November 2025, I successfully cleared three technical rounds, completed document verification, and even had salary discussions. Everything was finalized from my side, but I never received the offer letter.
* In 2026, I cleared another company’s technical round and also completed the final client round. However, till now, I have not received any update.

These repeated situations have been mentally exhausting and professionally damaging. I genuinely feel I have lost valuable time. Had I continued with my earlier opportunities, I would have gained almost 2.5–3 years of industry experience by now. Instead, I am still struggling to secure even a ₹20,000 per month role, despite initially aiming for a 5 LPA opportunity through Masai.

At this stage, I am also open to starting as an intern with a minimum stipend of ₹12,000 per month if that helps me enter the industry and gain practical experience. My priority now is to begin my professional journey and build real-world exposure.

Each time I hear that “the job market is tough,” but that does not change my personal reality. If I had independently focused on external opportunities earlier, I believe I could have secured employment much sooner. However, I trusted the Masai placement process and waited, which has significantly impacted my career timeline.

Therefore, I need clear and honest communication from your side:

1. What concrete and immediate steps will be taken to ensure my placement?
2. If Masai is no longer able to support me effectively, please communicate this transparently so I can plan my career independently without further delay.

This is not just about a job; it is about my time, trust, and future. I cannot continue waiting without clarity and accountability.

I request a direct and transparent response at the earliest.

Sincerely,
Ritesh Maurya
pw15_022

Message:
**Urgent Request: Adjustment of Exam Timing for March 8th and 15th (Ramadan)**
****
**I am writing to formally request a slight adjustment to the scheduled exam timings for the upcoming assessments on March 8th and March 15th.**
**Currently, the exams are set to begin at 6:30 PM. As these dates fall during the holy month of Ramadan, 6:30 PM coincides exactly with the time for Iftar (breaking of the fast) and the subsequent obligatory evening prayer.**
**Following the current schedule would make it extremely difficult for observant students to break their fast and perform their religious duties before the exam begins. Therefore, I earnestly request that the start time be moved from 6:30 PM to 7:30 PM for these two specific dates. This one-hour adjustment would allow us enough time to break our fast, pray, and arrive at the examination hall focused and ready.**
**I hope you will consider this request with empathy, as it would greatly help us balance our academic responsibilities with our religious observances.**
**Thank you for your time and understanding.**
****
****
**Best regards,**
**Ahsan Ullah Khan**

Title: Concerns Over Incomplete and Unclear Presentation Evaluation Process
Message:
Hi Masai Team,

I am writing to express my concern regarding the recent presentation evaluation conducted on 28th February at 6:30 PM.

Our assigned mentor for the session was Karan Purohit. We joined the meeting around 6:35 PM bec delay from host so waited in meet lobby. Once the presentation began, we covered the first 4–5 slides within approximately 5–6 minutes and then proceeded to demonstrate our MVP.

However, during the MVP demonstration, we were interrupted multiple times with questions before being allowed to complete the full walkthrough of either the MVP or the remaining slides. The session felt more like a short viva focused mainly on rapid questioning for about 10–15 minutes rather than a structured presentation review. The session concluded around 7 PM without our complete presentation or MVP being properly reviewed.

If neither the full MVP nor the complete 10-slide presentation content is seen, it becomes difficult to understand how a fair evaluation can be made based on just a few questions. A presentation evaluation should ideally assess the overall effort — including slide structure, clarity of explanation, thought process, problem understanding, and complete MVP demonstration — not only responses to 1–2 questions.

If the primary focus was intended to be only on the MVP or a viva-style assessment, it would have been helpful to inform us beforehand. We invested significant time in preparing both the presentation slides and the MVP demonstration with the expectation that both would be evaluated.

Additionally, I believe mentors who conduct the tutorial sessions may be in a better position to evaluate, as they are already familiar with the team's progress and efforts.

I am sharing this feedback respectfully with the intention of improving the evaluation process for future presentations.

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Thanks & regards,
Nancy Mishra

Title: Request Approval to Present MoneyMentor as Final Project
Message:
**Subject:** Request for Approval – Presenting Existing AI Product (MoneyMentor) as Final Project

Hi Team,

I hope you're doing well.

I’m writing to request approval to present my existing AI product, MoneyMentor, as my final project instead of building the assigned “Recruit-AI” case study.

Over the past few months, I have independently built MoneyMentor from scratch as a full AI product, applying the exact frameworks covered in this course — including problem discovery, user validation, RAG architecture, AI metrics, evaluation loops, and product lifecycle thinking.

Why MoneyMentor is a Strong Fit for the Course Objectives

1. Real-world Problem & Clear Persona
MoneyMentor addresses a genuine pain point: individuals struggling to understand and manage personal financial behavior using fragmented banking data and email alerts.

2. AI-Native Architecture
The system includes:

Gmail ingestion pipeline (real-world unstructured data)

Parsing + normalization layer

Vector embeddings & retrieval (RAG)

LLM-powered insights

Evaluation and optimization loops

3. Product Thinking Applied

Defined ICP and user personas

Identified measurable success metrics (engagement, insight quality, retention signals)

Designed architecture tradeoffs (cost vs accuracy, embedding vs fine-tuning)

Implemented guardrails & error handling

Iterated based on real constraints (rate limits, ingestion errors, cost efficiency)

4. Agentic + RAG Implementation
The product uses:

Retrieval-based reasoning

Structured + unstructured data fusion

Decision logic for insight generation

Production trade-offs (performance, cost, reliability)

This aligns strongly with the course focus on:

AI product lifecycle

RAG systems

Agentic workflows

Evaluation frameworks

Responsible AI considerations

Practical Constraint

As a full-time employee, building a completely new product within the limited timeframe may reduce the depth and quality of execution. Presenting MoneyMentor would allow me to demonstrate significantly deeper thinking, stronger architecture decisions, and real-world implementation challenges.

Proposal

I would structure the final presentation in the same format required for Recruit-AI:

Problem framing

Persona & pain points

AI system design

Evaluation strategy

Metrics

Risks & responsible AI

Title: Test Issues: Question Clarity, Timing, and Platform Lag
Message:
I wanted to raise a few issues I experienced during yesterday’s test.

1. Question Wording: Most of the questions were very long and difficult to understand. The wording felt confusing and overcomplicated, especially for case-based Product Management questions. Instead of testing understanding, a lot of time went into just figuring out what the question was actually asking.

2. Time Given: We had 30 minutes for 18 questions, which would have been manageable if the questions were concise. But given how lengthy and heavy they were, the time felt insufficient.

3. Platform Lag: After every second or third question, the next question took around 7–8 seconds to load. This kept happening throughout the test and wasted valuable time in an already tight window.

Overall, these issues made it difficult to attempt the test properly. I request that this be reviewed so future tests are clearer and the platform runs smoothly.

Please let me know how this will be addressed.

Title: Suggestion to Update Lecture and Assignment Numbering Format
Message:
Dear Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I would like to share a suggestion regarding the current lecture and assignment numbering format.

At present, the lectures are numbered in a continuous sequence across all modules. Earlier, when they were categorized module-wise, it was comparatively easier to track and identify the relevant content. With the current structure, it becomes slightly difficult to determine which lecture belongs to which module, especially while revising or referring back to specific topics.

If feasible, I would like to suggest restructuring the numbering format to reflect the module reference as well. For example:

Module 3, Lecture 5 → 3.5

Module 2, Assignment 4 → 2.4

This format would make navigation more intuitive and help learners quickly identify and organize their study material module-wise. A similar structure for assignments would also improve clarity and ease of reference.

Thank you for considering this suggestion. I truly appreciate the efforts being put into organizing the program content.

Best regards,
Parteek Gupta

I understand that this may affect my course GPA, and I take full responsibility for not being able to complete the project on time. Since the course is intended for both students and working professionals, the timeline felt very tight in this situation.

I truly value the course and the learning experience, and I regret that I could not submit the project as expected. I just wanted to formally inform you of the situation.

Dear Support Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I am writing regarding our upcoming examinations. As we are currently preparing, I would like to request a clear and detailed roadmap of the syllabus for the exam. It would be very helpful if you could clarify the exact topics to focus on and their weightage, if applicable.

Additionally, I would appreciate it if you could share:

* The exam pattern (objective/descriptive, number of sections, marking scheme, etc.)

Furthermore, I would like to know how the support team or faculty will assist us during this preparation phase. For example, will there be any revision classes, doubt-clearing sessions, mock tests, or additional study materials provided?

Your guidance and support in this regard will greatly help us in our exam preparation journey.

Looking forward to your response.

Best regards,
Netha

Title: Request for Accessible Materials and Support for Visually Impaired Student
Message:
Subject: Request for Accessibility Support and Downloadable Learning MaterialsDear Sir/Madam,I am a visually impaired student currently enrolled in the Agent System and Design course at Divya Sampark, IIT Roorkee. I am sincerely learning about agent systems and their design concepts under this program. As a student from an Arts background, I have recently entered the coding field, and I am eager to develop strong technical skills in this new domain.When I joined this course, I was informed that prior coding knowledge would not be mandatory for enrollment, as the course would accommodate students from non-technical backgrounds. This assurance encouraged me to register and begin my learning journey.However, I am facing certain challenges during the learning process. As a visually impaired student, I find it difficult to follow some of the coding and Python-related lectures due to my vision limitations. Additionally, because I need to travel from home daily, I am occasionally unable to attend the live sessions on time. These issues are affecting my ability to keep up with the course material.Therefore, I respectfully request that some additional facilities and support be provided to help me learn more effectively, such as:Access to download all course materials (lecture videos, study notes, e-books, and audio resources) for offline use.Audio-compatible or screen-reader-friendly study materials for visually impaired learners.Reasonable flexibility in assignment submissions, if possible, to accommodate accessibility challenges.I am fully committed to learning the Agent System and Design concepts with sincerity, and I truly wish to perform well in this program. With accessible materials and a few supportive measures, I am confident that I can overcome these challenges and achieve strong results.I kindly request the institute to consider my situation sympathetically and provide the necessary assistance to ensure equal learning opportunities for visually impaired students.Thank you very much for your understanding and continued support.Yours faithfully,
Ganesh

Title: Dispute Over Grading of Final Exam Question 94 Calculation
Message:
Hello Masai Team, 

I have a concern regarding a numerical question in the final exam that has been incorrectly graded for me. It is question 94. 

The question is - A neural network is being trained for 50 epochs. Training dataset contains 12000 samples, batch size is 120 samples and network updates the weights after each batch. Calculate the total number of weight updates that will occur during the complete training proces.

The correct answer is given as 5000. I arrived at the same conclusion. 

100 batches per epoch x 50 epochs = 5000.

However, this doesn't account for the fact that once training is complete, the weights will NOT be updated for the last batch. So in actuality the number of weight updates will be 

5000-1 = 4999. 

This is the answer i have entered. 

I request you to please consider this as a correct answer as well since it demonstrates critical reasoning in addition to direct math application. I hope you will take this into consideration.

Best regards,
Nayana Anil

Title: Request for Online Exam Due to Medical and Travel Constraints
Message:
I am writing to respectfully request consideration for appearing in the certification examination through an online mode due to unavoidable medical and logistical constraints.

The assigned offline examination center at Vanasthalipuram is approximately 120 kilometers from my current residence. Over the past week, I have been dealing with serious health issues, and in my current condition, undertaking such long-distance travel is medically difficult and not advisable. Given these circumstances, attending the examination in person would pose significant health challenges.

I would also like to clarify that I am not seeking placement opportunities through this program, and I request that I not be considered for any placement processes. I am currently working as a Business Intelligence Analyst with over 4 years of professional experience. My sole objective in completing this certification is to strengthen my academic profile for higher studies.

At the time of enrollment, I had inquired about the examination mode, and the offline-only format was not clearly communicated. I completely understand the academic standards and integrity associated with conducting an offline examination. However, I humbly request that my case be considered as an exceptional circumstance.

I have consistently performed well throughout the course, and my grades reflect my commitment and seriousness toward the program. We invested a significant amount in this certification, and being unable to complete it due to health-related travel constraints would be extremely unfortunate.

I am not requesting any relaxation in evaluation standards. I am fully willing to comply with strict online proctoring requirements to maintain academic integrity.

I sincerely request you to kindly consider my situation and allow me the opportunity to complete the certification through an online examination format.

Thank you very much for your time and understanding.

Message:
Dear Masai Team,

Many of us, including myself, come from non-technical backgrounds, so these topics are completely new. I respectfully request that you consider removing the last three topics from the examination. We are genuinely here to learn and develop a deep understanding of AI/ML concepts, and since this is a broad and complex field, it requires adequate time for proper comprehension. I have spoken to my batchmates, and we all feel that the pace is too rushed. Title: Clarification on Submission Format for Electricity Bill Calculator Task
Message:
Real-World Context:
When you follow a recipe to make tea, you follow steps like: "Boil water → Add tea leaves → Add sugar → Strain → Serve". Similarly, computer programs are just step-by-step instructions written in a way computers understand.

Your Task:
You are planning to create a simple Electricity Bill Calculator for your home. You will NOT write any code. Instead, you will create a detailed plan (like a recipe) that explains to a computer HOW to calculate an electricity bill.

What You Need to Submit:
Create a document (Google Docs, Word file, or PDF) that contains:

Part 1: Understanding the Problem (20 marks)
Write in your own words:

What information do we need from the user?
What calculations need to be done?
What result should be shown?
Part 2: Step-by-Step Instructions (40 marks)
Write the steps in simple English (like a recipe). For example:

Step 1: Ask the user "What is your name?"
Step 2: Ask the user "How many units of electricity did you use?"
Step 3: Calculate the bill using this formula:
Bill Amount = Units × 8 rupees per unit
Step 4: Check if bill is more than 500 rupees
If yes, add 50 rupees as extra charge
If no, keep bill as it is
Step 5: Show the final bill to the user `
Part 3: Apply Formulas (20 marks)
Show the math with actual numbers (like solving a problem):

Example Calculation:

Customer name: Rajesh Kumar
Units used: 75
Rate per unit: ₹8
Calculate:

Basic Bill = 75 × 8 = ₹600
Is bill > ₹500? Yes
Extra charge = ₹50
Final Bill = ₹600 + ₹50 = ₹650
Do 2 different examples like this with different numbers.

Part 4: Create a Flowchart (20 marks)
Draw a flowchart showing how the program flows. Use:

Oval for Start/End
Rectangle for actions (like "Calculate bill")
Diamond for decisions (like "Is bill > 500?")
Arrows to connect them

Title: Issues with Course Dashboard and Attendance Tracking Visibility
Message:
Dear Masai School Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I wanted to raise a concern regarding the recent changes in the course dashboard interface and attendance tracking system.

Initially, when I started the course, attendance was marked directly beside the topic itself. For example, if a specific topic was taught and I attended it, it was clearly marked as “Attended” or “Present” next to that topic. This made it very easy to track my progress.

However, now the interface seems to have changed. I can no longer see the attendance status beside the topic without opening it first, which is creating some confusion.

Additionally, when I open the dashboard, it first shows the Foundation Course. I then have to manually switch to my current course. Earlier, it used to clearly show which videos I had completed, and after 24 hours, the attendance would reflect as “Present” — which I understand is the system process. But now, even for previously watched videos, the “Present” status is not visible.

The new layout is making it difficult to:

* Track which lectures I have completed and which are pending
* View attendance status clearly
* Locate assignments easily
* Access lectures in a structured manner

The previous version was much simpler and more intuitive. The current version is causing some difficulty in navigation and tracking progress.

Kindly help me understand these changes and guide me on how to properly track attendance, lectures, and assignments in the updated system.

Looking forward to your support.

Thank you.

Best regards,
Lagan Prasad
